title: logformatter: strip "--url xxx" from bats podman-remote
body:
...in failure messages. It provides nothing but visual clutter.

Been testing a few days in #17831. Here's a [sample failure](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4717522784616448/html/sys-remote-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--00356). Hover cursor over `podman-remote` to get the url, should that be of any interest to anyone.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Well I am not against this change but I must say the fact the we use `--url` in CI tests but not when doing something like `hack/bats --remote` is extremely confusing. I don't know what purpose the --url serves in CI but it results in many workaround in tests that actually need to to test the --remote/--url/--connection options. This difference at least has already cost me several hours due the required debugging.
So maybe the better question is why does CI use --url at all? Should we just drop that?

That's my fault. The original socket dates back to varlink in 2019. When we ditched varlink in 2020 I unthinkingly changed the Makefile invocation without considering if the test socket was still needed. I will work on a fix.
